scenario: A Service Task is configured to call a partner-credit-check service. In Web Modeler, the modeler must specify which Job Worker handles it. The team has a worker registered with type credit-check-v2.
question: Which Service Task property binds the BPMN to that worker?

options.text: The name attribute on a BPMN element is a display label shown in the diagram canvas and in Operate â€” it serves a purely presentational purpose. Job Workers do not match on task name; they subscribe to the zeebe:taskDefinition type string. Setting the name to match a worker type string would only work coincidentally if someone programmed the worker to read task name, which is non-standard. The correct binding property is the type attribute on the zeebe:taskDefinition extension element, not the display name.

The processId identifies the deployed BPMN process definition as a whole â€” it is used to start new process instances or reference the process in Call Activities. It has no role in routing activated jobs to Job Workers. A Service Task produces jobs typed by its zeebe:taskDefinition type attribute. The processId operates at the process definition level, while task type operates at the individual task level â€” these are distinct layers of the Zeebe execution model with different routing purposes.

The id attribute on a BPMN element is the internal technical identifier used for graph connectivity (sequence flow references, boundary event attachments) and Operate element display. Job Workers do not subscribe by element id â€” they subscribe by the zeebe:taskDefinition type string. No correlation between element id and worker subscription exists in the Zeebe protocol. Using element id for worker binding would require non-standard custom code that reads task metadata at runtime.

zeebe:taskDefinition with type attribute set to credit-check-v2 is configured via the Implementation section in Web Modeler. Job Workers subscribe to a specific task type string; Zeebe routes activated jobs to workers subscribed to that exact type string. Setting the type to credit-check-v2 on the Service Task ensures only workers registered for credit-check-v2 receive and process those jobs. This is the canonical Zeebe binding mechanism between a BPMN Service Task and its executing Job Worker implementation. ###

a.text: Incorrect. zeebe:taskDefinition with type attribute is the canonical Zeebe extension. Workers subscribe to this type string; Zeebe routes jobs accordingly. Set via the Implementation property panel.

c.text: Incorrect. processId identifies the process, not task binding.

d.text: Correct. id is internal element ID; workers don't match on it.

explanation: id is internal element ID; workers don't match on it.
